title: [Feature]: Evaluate Apache Ignite and GridGain sinks
problem: Oracle GoldenGate connectivity material lists Apache Ignite and GridGain. nats-sinks does not yet define a sink strategy for in-memory data grid targets.
proposal: Research Apache Ignite and GridGain sink support, including whether the target can provide durable enough storage for ACK-gated operation or should only be used as a non-gated fan-out target. The design should evaluate SQL, key-value, thin client, persistence, cache configuration, and duplicate handling.
users: Teams using in-memory data grids for low-latency state propagation or operational data access.
delivery_semantics: ACK-gated success is allowed only when persistence and write confirmation provide a durable boundary. Volatile cache-only writes must be marked unsafe for critical event custody.
security: Cluster endpoints, credentials, cache names, keys, and payloads must be redacted. TLS and authentication must be verified by default when supported.
acceptance: Research determines durable versus non-durable deployment modes.
The design selects a client API and data model.
Configuration validates cache/table names, key strategy, timeouts, and persistence assumptions.
Unit tests use fakes and no live clusters.
Documentation clearly marks unsafe cache-only modes.
tests: Add fake client tests for key mapping, duplicate handling, persistence mode validation, and error classification.
documentation: Add Ignite/GridGain evaluation notes and update roadmap.
closeout: Close after release of the research or implementation.
